title: Retanilla trinervia
intro: Retanilla trinervia, also known as Chilean mint or Chilean yerba buena, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the relief of digestive issues, reduction of inflammation, and treatment of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Retanilla trinervia include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Retanilla trinervia is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, with tea being the most popular herbal preparation made from this plant.
taxonomy: Retanilla trinervia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to South America and is also known as the 'Three-veined Verbena'. The classification of Retanilla trinervia is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Lamiales. This plant is also known by its synonyms Verbena trinervis and Verbena trinervia. Further taxonomic classifications are necessary for a more detailed understanding of this species.

benefits: Retanilla trinervia, also known as Chilean pepper, is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
